activities_projects.title: Detailed Map Analysis of India
activities_projects.objective: Develop map reading and analysis skills
steps: Mark India's international boundaries on the outline map
Label all neighbouring countries
Write a short analysis of India's geopolitical location
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students develop advanced map interpretation skills

activities_projects.title: Study of India's Central Location
steps: Locate Europe, West Asia and East Asia on the world map
Observe India's central position among them
Explain how this location benefits India economically and culturally
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students analyze the significance of India's location

activities_projects.title: Comparative Study of Large Countries
activities_projects.objective: Compare India's size with other large countries
steps: Identify the seven largest countries in the world
Compare India's area with at least three of them
Write a brief comparison

activities_projects.title: Latitude and Climate Study
activities_projects.objective: Understand the impact of latitude on climate
steps: Observe the latitudinal extent of India
Identify how the Tropic of Cancer divides India
Explain how latitude influences climate
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students connect latitude with climatic variations

activities_projects.title: India and the Indian Ocean Trade
steps: Identify the Indian Ocean on the world map
Trace trade routes connecting Asia, Europe and Africa
Explain India's role in these trade routes

activities_projects.title: Cultural Exchange Through Trade
steps: Read about historical trade routes
Identify cultural exchanges such as ideas or architecture
Write a report describing their influence

activities_projects.title: Time Zone Study
steps: Identify the easternmost and westernmost longitudes of India
Calculate approximate time difference between them
Explain why India uses a single time zone

activities_projects.title: Indian Subcontinent Study
activities_projects.objective: Understand the concept of the Indian subcontinent
steps: Locate countries of the Indian subcontinent
List the countries included in this region
Explain why this region is called a subcontinent
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students understand the concept of a subcontinent

activities_projects.title: Sea Route Exploration
steps: Identify ancient sea routes used by traders
Mark routes connecting India with Africa and Southeast Asia
Explain how these routes influenced trade

activities_projects.title: Report on India's Geographical Importance
steps: Study India's location on the world map
Identify its advantages in trade, culture and communication
Write a detailed report summarizing your findings
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students critically analyze India's geographical importance
activities_projects.evaluation: Depth of analysis and clarity (10 marks)
